paragraphs.text: person. Secondly, no matter how spiritual someone is, he cannot help but give the intellectual and carnal their rightful place; he maintains just a little of them, in subordination to the spirit. Let intellectuality be not too broad within him (in scientific knowledge, arts and other subjects), and let carnality be firmly restrained—then he is a real, whole person. But the man of intellect (the expert, the connoisseur, the shrewd man)—and even more so the carnal man—is not a real person, no matter how appealing he seems outwardly. Fie is foolish. Hence the simple man who fears God is superior to the man who is diverse and elegant, but who does not have among his goals and yearnings the pleasing of God. You may judge in the same way about works of literature and art. Works in which everything is carnal are completely bad; those in which intellectuality is supreme also do not answer their purpose, although they are higher than the carnal. This judgment concerns those works that have no spiritual elements; those that are directly hostile toward all that is spiritual, that is, toward God and all Divine things, are direct suggestions of the enemy and should not be tolerated.

From this you see that according to natural purpose, man must live in the spirit, subordinate everything to the spirit, be penetrated by the spirit in all that is of the soul, and even more so in all that is physical—and beyond these, in the outward things, too, that is, family and social life. This is the norm!

I will not try to persuade you to live in the spirit and subordinate everything to it, supposing that if you have thoroughly examined everything that has been said, that the determination to live in this way already exists within you. You have already expressed the firm desire to stand on the level of human life as it is intended to be. You see now what it is intended to be, and of course, you wish it to be so. Yes, indeed, you have lived up to now in your family and circle of relatives in the manner depicted, that is, according to the spirit, although you 75

THE SPIRITUAL LIFE have studied sciences, know how to run a household, and are a master of music and singing. You do not have to be concerned with establishing the spiritual within yourself, but rather with supporting and maintaining it, protecting yourself from the influences and attractions of the intellectual-carnal life, into whose whirlpool you began to fall. This is the very thing we had agreed to discuss. Ofcourse, you are awaiting a solution as soon as possible to the question of how this life is to be. 13 LIFE ACCORDING TO THE SPIRIT The true happiness ofman is life according to the spirit. The subtle covering ofthe soul which serves as an intermediary between it and the body and as a means ofcommunication ofsouls between themselves and with the world ofsaints and angels.
The radiant and the dark state ofthe covering ofthe soul.
I wanted to answer the question at the end of the last letter, but your name day was approaching, and I thought I would send you my best wishes on the occasion.
I wish you above all good health, because that is the precondition for obtaining every blessing which man considers to be a blessing, and the delight and enjoyment of those blessings.

Really, for a sick and feeble person, which pleasures are possible, when all the senses are in an abnormal state? An exception should be made only for spiritual comforts. They do not depend in such a way on the state of one’s health, and they may be present even in spite of bodily sufferings. Martyrs, while undergoing the very process of martyrdom, truly rejoiced; actively, and not just with their tongues did they express their rejoicing. 7^

LIFE ACCORDING TO THE SPIRIT What should I wish you next? Usually one is wished happiness at this point. So I, too, wish you happiness. Just why do we wish this? Indeed, no one to this day has defined what happiness is, or who is truly happy. As I understand it, whoever considers himself to be happy is happy. Therefore, by wishing you happiness, I am also wishing you this. I wish you always to consider yourself to be happy! W^hy do I wish this? People have so many different conceptions and tastes concerning this, that you cannot make anything of it. But I would say to you confidentially, that as long as you are not living in the spirit, do not expect happiness. Intellectual and physical life, when the course is favorable, give something like happiness, but it is a fleeting illusion of happiness, and soon vanishes. Moreover, in their presence is a stormy area formed between soul and body by passionate thoughts, desires and aroused feelings; it is always powerful. With its presence, by reason of the poison of the passions, the only possibility is intoxication, which forgets suffering, the same as from opium; this forgetfulness ofsuffering does not come as the result of the absence of passions and sickness of heart. Sickness of heart constitutes an integral part of life in this world. The spirit however, soars beyond the boundaries of all troubles and carries away the person who abides in the spirit, and, by allowing him to taste its blessings, which are ever present, makes him truly and completely happy.

Well, then, shall I conclude my well-wishing with this? No, I do not think that is enough. If our life ended with this life, then, of course, it would be sufficient to say, “Be healthy and happy. As it does not end in this way, but continues even beyond the grave, and there only is the true life to be found, then, for the sake of completeness in my well-wishing, I cannot but wish that you be considered worthy and be blessed there.
May you be blessed in that place. With all my heart I wish you this, and diligently pray to the Lord: may He bring you the true 77
THE SPIRITUAL LIFE life by trouble-free means, and after that establish you in His ever blessed kingdom.

With this, of course, there arise very harsh conditions in your consciousness. There are, of course, crucial conditions, but are they really harsh? I will make a general comment on this for the time being: yes and no, judging by how an individual is set from childhood, and judging by that, I would say that for you, they are not very harsh. You are already carrying them out. Just be on your guard and do not retreat.
Another thought has come to me, to send you candy on your nameday, but I do not know if I can get any. I will try.

In case you have not forgotten, at one time I began talking with you about an exceedingly subtle element that is finer than light. It is called ether. Let us suppose that it exists as a matter of fact, and not just in name. I recognize that such a very subtle element exists, and that it penetrates and passes everywhere, serving as the smallest particle of material substance. Along with this, I suppose that within this element soar all the blessed spirits—the angels and Divine saints—^who are themselves garbed in a kind of raiment made of that very same element.

The covering of our soul (understand this word to refer also to the spirit, which is the soul of our human soul) is also made of this very same element. The soul itself is the immaterial spirit; but its covering is made from this ethereal immaterial element.
Our bodies are coarse; but that covering of the soul is very fine and serves as the intermediary between the soul and body.
Through it the soul acts on the body and the body on the soul.

I am speaking of this only in passing, however. Just keep in mind that the soul has this very fine covering, and that our souls have the very same kind of covering as do all spirits. From this it will not be hard for you to conclude that this omnipresent element, very fine, out of which these coverings are made and in which
